description: Virtual addressing with Container Table translation (v2+)

raw_analysis_notes: Translation formula verified: PLCN = map[VLCN >> shift] + (VLCN & mask). Tested on 39 images, all configurations

description: Metadata checksums: None (3.4), CRC64 (3.14 default), SHA256 (optional). User data checksums: opt-in via integrity streams
static_analysis_notes: Win10: 56 CRC template specializations (CmsCrc32/CmsCrc64) + CmsChecksumNone(8) — but CmsChecksumNone::VerifyChecksum always returns TRUE (no actual verification). Win11: unified CmsChecksum(11) delegates to SymCrypt. CmsIntegrityState (17/66 funcs). Literature claim 'always on metadata' CORRECTED: no metadata checksums on 3.4. Merged from GN_CHKS_SA_001 and GN_MISC_008
raw_analysis_notes: VBR 0x2A selector: 0=None(3.4), 2=CRC64(3.14), 4=SHA256. Win10 CONFIRMED no metadata checksums (fsutil: CHECKSUM_TYPE_NONE, CHKP bit 0x400 not set). Upgraded 3.4→3.14 gains CRC64 at CHKP level despite VBR 0x2A remaining 0x00. See report_checksum_investigation.md

description: 0x40-0x50: table identifier (high+low)
static_analysis_notes: E2 2026-06-18: FormatPageHeaderInternal writes page+0x40=id+0x178 (TableIdHigh), page+0x48=id+0x180 (TableIdLow); MsInitializeWellKnownObjectId builds the 16-byte _SmsBigIdentifier as {high=0, low=numeric OID}. Driver compares the LOW half (0x48); TableIds per A.4 (Object ID Table=0x02, Block Refcount=0x05, system tables 0x01-0x22) — E52. page+0x40 is the OID HIGH half (always 0), NOT a schema field. v3.4+v3.14.
raw_analysis_notes: RE-MEASURED ALL-DISK 2026-06-18: page+0x40==0 on 137,444/137,444 MSB+ pages (all versions/cluster sizes + SUPB/CHKP); numeric table OID at 0x48. The '0x40-0x50 = table identifier high+low' framing is CONFIRMED (16-byte OID); the earlier 'low=schema type' note was wrong. Resolves the NEEDS-STATIC hold. See structure_reference A.2.

description: Root 0x18-0x20: number of extents

raw_analysis_notes: CONFIRMED ALL-DISK 2026-06-19: desc+0x18 = number of leaf extents/pages (0 single-page; == leaf-page census on every clean multi-level table: oid7=10, oid73f=3, oid760=1). PRADE 'number of extents' CORRECT. See structure_reference A.2b. (Was NEEDS-STATIC in re-verify.)

description: Root 0x20-0x28: number of rows
static_analysis_notes: E2 2026-06-19: _SmsIndexRoot+0x20 (u64) = TOTAL row count of the whole table, incremented per row insert (EnqueueTreeUpdate this+0x38; CreateIndex win10 @73243; write-back @82620).
raw_analysis_notes: CORRECTED 2026-06-19 (FRAME ERROR REVERSED, was CONTRADICTED): Prade's '0x20-0x28 = number of rows' is CORRECT - it is the _SmsIndexRoot descriptor (page+0x50) +0x20 = whole-table row count. RD: desc+0x20 == independent leaf-walk total on 34,177/34,207 tables, diverging from the per-node _SmsIndexHeader+0x14 on multi-level trees (387 vs 10 children; 245,759 vs 2). The 2026-06-18 'CONTRADICTED / count at thoff+0x14' analysed the per-node NODE header (wrong frame). See errata E49, structure_reference A.2b.

description: 0x2A-0x37: 14 bytes unknown in Nordvik/Prade. 0x2A=checksum algo selector, 0x2B=reserved, 0x2C-0x2F=volume flags (0x06 Win10 / 0x66 Win11), 0x30-0x37=reserved
static_analysis_notes: All builds: 0x2A=checksum algorithm selector (1 byte; insider calls it 'boot option'); 0x2C=volume flags (4 bytes; insider names bits 5-6 'boot behavior'). Cluster factor validated as 0x80/0x100/0x200. Merged from FS_VBR_SA_001 (cross-build static analysis)
raw_analysis_notes: 0x2A confirmed as checksum algorithm selector: 0=None(3.4), 2=CRC64(default 3.14), 4=SHA256. Immutable on upgrade: Win10-formatted volumes always 0=None even after 3.14 upgrade. Volume flags at 0x2C: Win10=0x06 (bits 0x02+0x04) vs Win11=0x66 (bits 0x02+0x04+0x20+0x40). Bit 0x02=mount marker (set by driver on first mount, RefsMountVolume decompiled). Bit 0x04=always present on valid ReFS. Bit 0x10=recovery/dirty (decompiled but never observed). Bit 0x20=Win11 format-time boot behavior. Bit 0x40=gates VBR 0x2A checksum algo (insider: 'used only if flags bit 6 set'). 0x30-0x37 always zero (reserved). See report_boot_sector_deep_analysis.md | RE-VERIFIED 2026-06-18 (all-disk): claimed flags are 0x06(Win10)/0x66(Win11); disk shows 0x26 is the genuine v3.7/v3.9 value (already documented in structure_reference Subtable A.1c), so the two-value summary in FS_VBR_012 is wrong/incomplete.

description: Type 0x20 is the per-object FileId-resolution index (RefsFindFileId/RefsCreateFileId2), NOT a directory reverse-lookup. Key = 0x80000020 + 0 + FileId(u64) + 0 (24B). Format A (type_flag=0, variable) = filename, for non-resident files. Format B (fixed 24B, type_flag=1 on v3.4 / =2 on v3.7+) = {tf, 0, FileId, home-dir back-ref OID}, for subdirectories + cross-dir links. Format B present on v3.4 (as tf=1); tf=1->tf=2 transition at v3.7.

description: Log Record Header: 56 bytes, checksum
static_analysis_notes: RE-ATTRIBUTED: the '56 bytes + checksum' is the ENTRY HEADER (Layer 2) at record+0x78 — 56 bytes (64 on Insider) with checksum (8-byte XOR-fold) at entry+0x08 (=page+0x80). Distinct from the equally-56-byte _SmsRedoRecord (Layer 4), which has NO checksum (record_size@+0x00 opcode@+0x04 table_key_path_length@+0x08 object_id@+0x20 flags@+0x2C). The prior note 'per-record checksum not found' is SUPERSEDED — the checksum IS per-record at entry+0x08.
raw_analysis_notes: probe_logcore_record.py: entry+0x08 checksum varies per record (win11 0xfa2a568b/0x163223eb/0x1becc9a9) while page+0x04 magic is constant. Inner _SmsRedoRecord still >= 0x38. See finding #319.

Lee's v3.4 opcodes: 0x01=InsertRow 0x04=UpdateDataWithRoot 0x00=OpenTable — plausible CREATE pattern. refs_mlog.py --parse classifies CREATE from OpenTable+InsertRow+SetParentId. Verification possible on v3.4 images.
raw_analysis_notes: Lee's pattern matches WRITE transactions (163x on 3 v3.4 images): INSERT UPD_DATA SET_OBJREC OPEN UPD_DATA INSERT OPEN = 0x01 0x04 0x10 0x00 0x04 0x01 0x00. 6/7 opcodes match Lee (position 3: 0x10 vs Lee's 0x01). Actual CREATE is a separate prior transaction: OPEN OPEN UPD_DATA SET_OBJREC INSERT INSERT INSERT SET_PARENT (35x). Lee likely described create-with-content as single operation.

description: File deletion: 0x0F->0x02->0x0F->0x02->0x04
static_analysis_notes: Lee's v3.4 opcodes: 0x0F=DeleteTable 0x02=DeleteRow 0x04=UpdateDataWithRoot — plausible DELETE. forefst mlog classifies DELETE only from RedoDeleteTable (0x0F); a bare DeleteRow (0x02) is ENTRY_REMOVE — a rename/move old-name removal not a deletion (2026-07-03 classifier).
raw_analysis_notes: Core pattern DEL_TABLE DELETE DEL_TABLE DELETE = 0x0F 0x02 0x0F 0x02 observed 23x across 3 v3.4 images. Exact first-4 match. Lee's trailing 0x04 (timestamp update) appears as separate MODIFY transaction in our images.

description: File modification: 0x06->0x04->0x04->0x04->0x04->0x08
static_analysis_notes: Lee's v3.4 opcodes: 0x06=Allocate 0x04=UpdateDataWithRoot 0x08=SetRangeState(gap). 0x08 is a gap in v3.4 dispatch. Pattern includes opcodes not in PerformRedo dispatch table.
raw_analysis_notes: 6-opcode sequence NOT found as single transaction in any v3.4 image. Individual components observed as separate transactions: ALLOC UPD_DATA (36x) + UPD_DATA (205x) + SET_RANGE (97x). Lee may have used different transaction boundary definition or different v3.4 build. Confidence lowered to Medium.

description: File renaming: 0x02->0x05->0x01->0x04->0x04
static_analysis_notes: Lee's v3.4 opcodes: 0x02=DeleteRow 0x05=ReparentTable 0x01=InsertRow 0x04=UpdateDataWithRoot. Pattern contains REPARENT so this is a MOVE (cross-directory) not same-directory rename.
raw_analysis_notes: First 4 opcodes DELETE REPARENT INSERT UPD_DATA match our MOVE pattern (23x): DELETE REPARENT INSERT UPD_DATA SET_OBJREC UPD_DATA INSERT. Core 0x02 0x05 0x01 0x04 confirmed. Lee's 'renaming' is actually a move operation. Same-directory rename uses DELETE DELETE INSERT INSERT (no REPARENT). REFINEMENT 2026-07-03 (RD step6 v3.14): a same-dir RENAME DOES carry RedoReparentTable (0x05) on v3.14 so reparent-presence is NOT a version-robust move/rename signal; forefst mlog now distinguishes them by the parent-directory table OID (old-name DeleteRow.target_oid vs new-name InsertRow.target_oid — same=RENAME diff=MOVE) validated 100% vs FSActivity+USN; the v3.4 'rename=no reparent' pattern was not re-observed (0 renames in the v3.4 corpus images tested — all name-changes were OID-decidable moves).

description: Directory creation: 0x00->0x00->0x04->0x10->...
static_analysis_notes: Lee's v3.4 opcodes: 0x00=OpenTable 0x04=UpdateDataWithRoot 0x10=SetObjectRecordPayload — plausible CREATE. refs_mlog.py --parse classifies CREATE from OpenTable+InsertRow+SetParentId.
raw_analysis_notes: EXACT MATCH. All 35 CREATE transactions across 3 v3.4 images begin OPEN OPEN UPD_DATA SET_OBJREC = 0x00 0x00 0x04 0x10. Full sequence is 0x00 0x00 0x04 0x10 0x01 0x01 0x01 0x0E (Lee's '...' = INSERT INSERT INSERT SET_PARENT).

description: Directory deletion: 0x02->0x02->0x0F->0x02->...
static_analysis_notes: Lee's v3.4 opcodes: 0x02=DeleteRow 0x0F=DeleteTable — plausible DELETE. refs_mlog.py --parse classifies DELETE from DeleteTable/DeleteRow.
raw_analysis_notes: Same opcodes {DELETE DEL_TABLE} observed but ordering differs. Dominant pattern (23x): DEL_TABLE DELETE DEL_TABLE DELETE = 0x0F 0x02 0x0F 0x02. Lee: DELETE DELETE DEL_TABLE DELETE = 0x02 0x02 0x0F 0x02. Same opcode composition different ordering — record order within a transaction may vary by context.

description: Directory renaming: 0x02->0x02->0x01->0x01->0x04->0x04
static_analysis_notes: Lee's v3.4 opcodes: 0x02=DeleteRow 0x01=InsertRow 0x04=UpdateDataWithRoot — same-directory rename (no REPARENT unlike AP_EVNT_004).
raw_analysis_notes: Core pattern DELETE DELETE INSERT INSERT = 0x02 0x02 0x01 0x01 observed 22x across 3 v3.4 images (11x with trailing SET_PARENT + 11x without). Exact first-4 match. Lee's trailing 0x04 0x04 (timestamp updates) appear as separate MODIFY transactions.

description: CHKP flags (0x78-0x7B) full decomposition: bit 0x002=always set, bit 0x080=native Win11 format (format-time only), bit 0x200=indirect root list mode, bit 0x400=CRC64 metadata checksumming, bits 0x010+0x020+0x100=dedup-specific. 4 observed composite values: 0x002(Win10), 0x602(upgraded), 0x682(native Win11), 0x7b2(Win11+dedup)
static_analysis_notes: ValidateCheckpointRecord: flags at CHKP+0x78 checked for 0x200 (indirect root mode) and other bits. See static_analysis_main_v2.md
raw_analysis_notes: Flags 0x682 for native Win11 vs 0x602 for upgraded Win10. 0x7b2 for dedup volumes. Bit 0x080 absent on upgraded volumes = format-time flag for native 3.14. Bit 0x200 controls root pointer parsing mode (direct vs indirect). Bit 0x400 correlates with CRC64 metadata checksumming (from ValidateCheckpointRecord SA). Bits 0x010/0x020/0x100 appear only on dedup-enabled volumes. See report_checkpoint_deep_analysis.md

description: MLog data pages = LogCore records with a 0x78 (120)-byte header [sig@0x00 + magic@0x04 (constant-per-volume NOT a CRC; see E42) + version@0x08 + log_block_size@0x0C + UUID@0x10 + counter@0x20 + LSN@0x28 + prevLSN@0x30 + length-in-4K-blocks@0x38/0x3C + entry_offset@0x54=0x78]. CORRECTED from "56-byte (CRC...)"
static_analysis_notes: LogCoreWriteDataRecord/LogInitializeEntryHeader build the 0x78 header; 0x04=magic from handle+0x2fe8 validated by equality (not CRC); real checksum = XOR-fold at entry+0x08 (page+0x80). v3.4/v3.14/Insider identical
raw_analysis_notes: probe_logcore_record.py RD-verified on 10 images (v3.4/3.7/3.14/upgraded/compression/262K/Insider-4K+64K). 0x04 constant per volume (win11 0x84c1ffcf; win10 0x998da6fd); entry+0x08 checksum varies per record; LSN chains prevLSN[n]==LSN[n-1] pre-wrap

description: Log wrapping detection: record_index can exceed physical page count in data area. Circular buffer confirmed

description: MLog data area LCNs (from OID 0x9 key=1) are physical — no container translation needed. Entire MLog operates in physical address space.
static_analysis_notes: CmsDurableLog reads physical LCNs directly without CmsVolumeContainer translation. OID 0x9 Logfile Information Table stores data area range as physical clusters.
raw_analysis_notes: refs_mlog.py reads data area pages at physical offsets (no tr.tr() call). All 25 baseline images parse correctly. Discovery contradicted implementation plan which assumed container translation was required.

description: CHKP 0x88-0x8B: data area end offset. Value = first_root_offset + root_count * ref_size. 0x680 for 3.4 (13x104B refs from 0x138), 0x380 for 3.14 (13x48B refs from 0x110)

raw_analysis_notes: Mathematically verified: 3.4: 0x138+13*104=0x680. 3.14: 0x110+13*48=0x380. Serves as bounds check for driver. See report_checkpoint_deep_analysis.md | RE-VERIFIED 2026-06-18 (all-disk): CHKP+0x88 data-area-end is NOT 'first_root_offset + root_count*ref_size'. v3.4: disk=0x680 but 0x94+13*0x68=0x5dc (mismatch). v3.14: disk=0x380/0x388 but 0x94+13*0x30=0x304. The headline VALUES (0x680 v3.4, ~0x380 v3.14, ~0x4d0 SHA256) are

raw_analysis_notes: Win10: 0x00000000. Win11 native and upgraded: 0x000E0003 (=3.14 packed as major|minor). See report_checkpoint_deep_analysis.md

Bootstrap roots {7 8 12} hardcoded in ValidateCheckpointRecord

Bootstrap roots {12,7,8} confirmed as literal array {0xC,7,8} in the ValidateCheckpointRecord lambda (win11 refs_win11.decomp.c:217186 / win11latest:217188 / insider:285225), consumed by a 3-iteration GetGlobalTableRoot revalidation loop; the roots-7/8/12 real-LCN fact is raw-disk-confirmed. Non-VBR parts (indirect roots, compact refs, OID changes) are out of this region's scope -- belo

description: CHKP flag 0x2000: Windows Insider only (build 29574+). Present on all insider-formatted and insider-upgraded disks. Absent on Win11 24H2 (build 26200). Related to CmsVolumeAttestation/CmsRollbackProtection
static_analysis_notes: 6 insider images all have 0x2000 set
raw_analysis_notes: 6 insider images all have 0x2000 set. See report_checkpoint_deep_analysis.md

description: Object Table row value header decoded: 8xu32 (format=2, reserved, key_off=0x18, val_off=0x30, payload_offset@u32[4], payload_length@u32[5], generation, dirty_gen) + LCN tuple (4xQ) + checksum. B+-tree value_length = 240/248 (legacy) or 80/88 (compact, growing to 104/112 under SHA-256), the compact form introduced at v3.10; upgraded volumes carry mixed sizes.
static_analysis_notes: CmsObjectTable::GetObjectRecordOfIdentifier and MsGetObjectRecordPayload operate on this row format
raw_analysis_notes: value+0x10 = payload OFFSET, constant per size-family: 200 legacy / 80 compact / 104 compact-SHA-256. value+0x14 = payload length (0 or 8). off+len = driver-copyable payload extent (200/208 legacy) per CmsObjectTable::GetObjectRecordPayload @140118c14 (src = value+u32@0x10; copy u32@0x14 bytes). value_length = payload extent + a 40-byte tail on legacy (240/248); compact has no tail (value_length = off+len). Generation counter at u32[6] = checkpoint virtual clock. All-disk verified 2026-07-06: 34603 OT rows across 114 ReFS images, 0 violations; off@0x10 flat within each family; compact size tracks checksum size (80/88 default, 104/112 SHA-256), independent of cluster size.

description: OID 0xD = Trash Table (CmsTrashTable). Async deleted-file cleanup queue with schema 0xe0d0. CmsTrashTable::InitializeTable sets OID=0xD and calls MsCreateDurableTableObject. Empty (0 rows) on clean volumes.
static_analysis_notes: CmsTrashTable::InitializeTable decompiled: local_28=0xd, schema=0xe0d0. Win10+Win11 refs.sys both contain CmsTrashTable class
raw_analysis_notes: OID 0xD present on all 48 images. B+ tree at OID 0xD shows 0 rows on clean volumes. Page has valid MSB+ signature

description: OID 0x501 = Volume Information Table (failover/duplicate). Created alongside OID 0x500 by InitializeVolumeInfoTable using MsCreateDurableFailoverTableObject with schema 0x150 and count=2.
static_analysis_notes: InitializeVolumeInfoTable decompiled: MsInitializeWellKnownObjectId(0x500, 0x501), schema=0x150, MsCreateDurableFailoverTableObject
raw_analysis_notes: OID 0x501 present on all 48 images. Same failover pattern as 0x07/0x08 and 0x09/0x0A

description: OIDs 0x540/0x541 = Reparse Point Index (primary/failover). Created by InitializeReparseIndexTable using MsCreateDurableFailoverTableObject with schema 0x160. References REFS_REPARSE_INDEX_FILE_NAME. Indexes reparse points by tag+OID for efficient enumeration.
static_analysis_notes: InitializeReparseIndexTable decompiled: MsInitializeWellKnownObjectId(0x540, 0x541), schema=0x160, ReparseIndexFileReference string
raw_analysis_notes: OIDs 0x540/0x541 present on all 48 images. Follows same failover pair pattern

description: Schema 0xe0d0 = Trash Table. Created by CmsTrashTable::InitializeTable with OID 0xD. Present on all versions (3.4+).
static_analysis_notes: CmsTrashTable::InitializeTable decompiled: schema=0xe0d0
raw_analysis_notes: Schema 0xe0d0 present on all 48 images

description: Schema 0xe110 = Read Cache Metadata. Created by CmsReadCache::ResetReadCacheMetadataFile. Present since v3.4 (v3.4-Insider).
static_analysis_notes: CmsReadCache::ResetReadCacheMetadataFile uses MsCreateDurableTableObject with 0xe110
raw_analysis_notes: Schema 0xe110 absent on v3.4. Present on v3.7+ images | RE-VERIFIED 2026-06-18 (all-disk): claim: '0xe110 ... First appears in v3.7 (Win11 21H2)' — disk: 0xe110 present on every v3.4 image (13/13), i.e. since v3.4, NOT first at v3.7

description: Schema 0xe130 = Heat Engine Persistence Table. CmsVolumeHeatEngine::Initialize creates table via MsCreateDurableTableObject. Persists container heat metrics for tiered storage optimization (compression/decompression/recompression heat classes). Found in Insider refs.sys binary only (not in Win11 26H1 build). First appears v3.10.
static_analysis_notes: CmsVolumeHeatEngine::Initialize in Insider refs.sys: mov dword [rsp+0x30] 0xe130 as param to MsCreateDurableTableObject. Related: Persist LoadFromTable TrimPersistedEntries
raw_analysis_notes: Schema 0xe130 absent on v3.4/v3.7. Present on v3.10+. Always 0 rows on test volumes (no active tiered storage)

description: Schema 0x1b0 = $SNAPSHOT/$NAMED_STREAM attribute (NOT Index Root). Confirmed via RefsQueryStreamsInfo: uVar5 = 0xb0 alongside RtlInitUnicodeString($SNAPSHOT). Type code 0xB0 exists in all driver builds but schema 0x1B0 only registered on disk at v3.14+.
static_analysis_notes: RefsQueryStreamsInfo sets type code 0xb0 with L$SNAPSHOT. 1228 entries across 46 images (28 true snapshots on 9 images + ADS entries). Schema registered in Schema Table only on v3.14+
raw_analysis_notes: Type code in driver since v3.4 but schema table entry only on v3.14+ disk images

description: Schemas 0x1c0/0x1d0 = $REPARSE_POINT (0xC0) / $EA_INFORMATION (0xD0). NTFS-equivalent type codes reused by ReFS. Min resident 8B. v3.7+. 0xC0: RefsGetReparsePointValue calls LookupAttribute(0xc0). 0xD0: RefsLookupEasOnFile(Ea.c) calls LookupAttribute(0xd0) with _EA_INFORMATION* param.
static_analysis_notes: RefsGetReparsePointValue: LookupAttribute(0xc0). RefsCreateReparsePointInternal: CreateAttributeWithValue(0xc0). RefsLookupEasOnFile: LookupAttribute(0xd0), param3=_EA_INFORMATION*
raw_analysis_notes: Schemas 0x1c0/0x1d0 absent on v3.4. Present on v3.7+. No instances in directory B+ trees on 80+ images — stored in per-file attribute sets, not visible in Object Table directory entries

description: Schema 0x1e0 = $EA (0xE0). Schema 0x1f0 (type 0xF0) = USN change-journal $Max metadata (NTFS $UsnJrnl:$Max: MaximumSize/AllocationDelta/UsnId/LowestValidUsn), an unnamed single-instance attr on the "Change Journal" file under OID 0x520 — NOT EFS and NOT $LOGGED_UTILITY_STREAM. CORRECTION: 0xF0 is created by RefsSetupUsnJournal (NOT RefsCreateLoggedUtilityStream, which uses type 0x100), the type-0xF0 ATTRIBUTE code path is v3.4-era (RefsSetupUsnJournal), but the registered SCHEMA entry 0x1f0 is v3.14+ — disk-confirmed absent on all v3.4/v3.7/v3.9/v3.10 schema tables and present on all v3.14/Insider; EFS uses the separate 0x100/0x200 path. Min 32B payload (0x20) + 12B wrapper = 44B.
static_analysis_notes: RefsLookupEasOnFile: LookupAttribute(0xe0). 0xF0: RefsSetupUsnJournal CreateAttribute(0xf0,NULL,0x20) win11 L445618 / win10 RefsCreateAttributeWithValue(0xf0,0x20) L325274; READ via LookupAttribute(0xf0,NULL). RefsMapLoggedUtilityStream/EfsReadEfsData use type 0x100, not 0xF0.
raw_analysis_notes: Type 0xF0 observed on exactly 9 images (1 per image, all where USN journal active), always on the Change Journal file under OID 0x520, marker 0x80000001, 44 bytes. MaxSize 32MB/128MB = standard fsutil usn createjournal sizes. v3.14 empirical floor is a sampling artifact (no pre-v3.14 corpus image had USN active).

description: Complete embedded attribute taxonomy with dual-marker system. The genuine embedded-attribute type-code set is EXACTLY 10 codes: 0x38/0x39 ($OBJ_LINK), 0x80 ($DATA), 0x90 ($I30_INDEX), 0xB0 ($SNAPSHOT/ADS), 0xC0 ($REPARSE), 0xD0 ($EA_INFO), 0xE0 ($EA), 0xF0 (USN $Max), 0x100 ($EFS); 0x50/0x60/0xA0 NEVER appear as embedded sub-records. Marker 0x80000001: single-instance (0x80 DATA header/0xC0 REPARSE/0xD0 EA_INFO/0xE0 EA / 0xF0 USN $Max). Marker 0x80000002: multi-instance (0x39 FileRef/0x80 DATA stream/0xB0 Named Stream/0x100 $EFS). 0xB0 format: val[0x04]=0x68=EXTENDED/non-inline (snapshots OR a large >=2KB ADS) vs !=0x68=INLINE (small ADS); val[0x10] is the ADS(0)-vs-snapshot(2) discriminator (E61). 532503 entries (526454 with markers + 6049 unclassified) across 409185 files on 112 images.
refs_version: all (core types 0x39/0x80 since v3.4; 0xF0 USN $Max since v3.4; 0xC0/0xD0 schema v3.7+; 0xB0/0xE0/0x100 schema v3.14+)
static_analysis_notes: RefsQueryStreamsInfo: type 0xb0 = $SNAPSHOT. RefsGetReparsePointValue: 0xC0. RefsLookupEasOnFile: 0xD0/0xE0. RefsSetupUsnJournal CreateAttribute(0xf0) (USN $Max). EFS uses type 0x100. Dual-marker system: 0x80000001 single-instance (incl 0xF0) / 0x80000002 multi-instance
raw_analysis_notes: 28 true version snapshots (dsid>0x1000) across 9 images + 1192 inline ADS + 2200 reparse points + 10130 EA entries (WSL $LXUID/$LXGID/$LXMOD/$LXDEV) + 9 type-0xF0 USN $Max (1/image). All schemas 0x1B0-0x200 empirically confirmed. Tool: refs_snapshots.py + dual_marker_scan.py

description: ReFS file deletion flow: RefsDeleteFile → MsDeleteRow (removes from directory B+ tree) → MsReparentFileToTrash → CmsTrashTable::AddFileTable (OID 0xD queues for cleanup) → background TrashCleanerWorkItemMethod → DeleteFileTable (frees data extents). CoW ensures old directory pages persist. Recovery: (1) Trash Table scan (2) checkpoint comparison (3) orphaned MSB+ page scan.
static_analysis_notes: RefsDeleteFile RefsDeleteFileId2 MsDeleteRow MsReparentFileToTrash CmsTrashTable::AddFileTable TrashCleanerWorkItemMethod CmsTrashTable::DeleteFileTable in Insider refs.sys
raw_analysis_notes: All 66 test images: empty trash tables and identical checkpoints (no recent deletions). 1222 orphaned leaf pages on millionsofactions image but contain allocator rows not directory entries. Tool: refs_deleted.py

description: VBR 0x58-0x1FF: 424-byte trailing area confirmed all-zero on 13 representative images spanning all ReFS versions (3.4-3.14) and configurations (4K/64K/128K clusters, CRC64, SHA256, dedup, insider). No hidden data. True reserved space.
static_analysis_notes: InitializeVcbFromBootSector reads only up to 0x57; nothing beyond accessed
raw_analysis_notes: Verified on 13 images: win10refs2g win10refs64k win11refsmini win11refs4gattributes win11refs64k win10to11refsupgrade win11refsdedup win11refssha256 win1121h2test win1122h2test win1123h2test insiderefs8g winsider. See ra_step4_12_deep_structure_report.md

description: SUPB page header fully decoded (0x00-0x4F). Page header version always 2. Reserved fields at 0x08 and 0x18 always zero. Volume signature = XOR of GUID dwords. LCN slots 1-3 always zero (SUPB is single-cluster). Table OID = 0. Gap 0x60-0x67 between Volume GUID and SUPB version confirmed always zero.
static_analysis_notes: CmsVolume::Start reads and validates SUPB page header fields
raw_analysis_notes: Page header fields verified on 13 images. SUPB 0x60-0x67 zero across all configurations. See ra_step4_12_deep_structure_report.md

description: CHKP 0x7C-0x87: 12-byte reserved field. Prade Table 4.7 labeled these as 'Offset to table references' (0x7C) 'Table reference count' (0x80) and 'Offset to unknown buffer' (0x84). All three are always zero on all 66 images tested (Prade correction).
static_analysis_notes: Field not accessed by ValidateCheckpointRecord — confirmed reserved
raw_analysis_notes: Verified zero on 13 representative + previously confirmed on 48 images (Step 4.5). See ra_step4_12_deep_structure_report.md

description: CHKP 0x8C: 0x20 (32) OR 0x0 — NOT always 32. CORRECTED 2026-06-18: it co-varies with CHKP+0x88 (both populated=0x20 / both zero=0x0); zeroed on native-v3.14 checkpoints. So '+0x88/+0x8C' is a field-pair, not a static 'max root capacity = 32' constant (no E2 ties it to MS_CHECKPOINT_MAX_ROOTS as a literal).
static_analysis_notes: RE-MEASURED 2026-06-18: CHKP+0x8C distinct values {32,0}; 56/226 checkpoint reads (= 28 native-v3.14 images x 2 slots) read 0, joint with 0x88=0. Matches structure_reference §A.4 / finding #339. The earlier 'always 0x20' was RD on a corpus that never observed the zeroed case.
raw_analysis_notes: See structure_reference §A.4, finding #339; both-zero on native v3.14.

description: Page header LCN tuple (0x20-0x38): MSB+ pages store 4 consecutive cluster LCNs (self self+1 self+2 self+3) for their 4-cluster extent. SUPB and CHKP use only slot 0 (self-block); slots 1-3 are zero. This is because SUPB is fixed at LCN 30 and CHKP locations are stored in SUPB checkpoint references.
static_analysis_notes: Page allocation code writes 4 LCNs at page header +0x20 for multi-cluster pages
raw_analysis_notes: Verified on 13 MSB+ root table pages from Win11 4K image: all have LCN[1]=Self+1 LCN[2]=Self+2 LCN[3]=Self+3. SUPB/CHKP confirmed zero at slots 1-3. See ra_step4_12_deep_structure_report.md

description: Page header Table OID (0x48): MSB+ pages store the Object ID of their owning table (e.g. 0x02=Object ID Table 0x0B=Container Table 0x22=Small Allocator). Enables identification of orphaned CoW-discarded pages. SUPB and CHKP have Table OID = 0.
static_analysis_notes: CmsBPlusTable node initialization writes owning table OID at page +0x48
raw_analysis_notes: Verified on 13 root table MSB+ pages: Table OID matches expected table identity. OID values observed: 0x02 0x03 0x04 0x05 0x06 0x07 0x08 0x09 0x0A 0x0B 0x0C 0x0D 0x22. See ra_step4_12_deep_structure_report.md

description: OID 0x530 B+ tree key/value structure decoded. Key (16B): value_size(4)+padding(4)+SecurityId_high(4)+SD_hash(4). Value: hash_echo(4)+count_echo(4)+size_echo(4)+SECURITY_DESCRIPTOR_RELATIVE. SD is self-relative Windows format with Owner/Group SIDs and DACL/SACL.
static_analysis_notes: CmsSecurityDescriptorStream and CmsHashTable classes in refs.sys. InitializeSecurityDescriptorStream creates OID 0x530
raw_analysis_notes: Parsed on win11refsmini.raw (12 SDs), win10refs2g.raw (8 SDs), win11refs4gattributes.raw (10 SDs). All SDs have revision=1 SE_SELF_RELATIVE. Tool: refs_security.py

description: Per-file SecurityId (at $SI+0x28 = entry offset 0x50) is compound u64: (reference_count << 32) | sd_hash. Lookup in OID 0x530 by matching key[8:12]==SecurityId_high and key[12:16]==SecurityId_low. CmsHashTable provides O(1) hash-based lookup.
static_analysis_notes: CmsHashTable class (18-23 functions) implements hash-based SD lookup
raw_analysis_notes: Cross-verified: file SecurityId 0x10a9f9562 correctly maps to SD with hash 0x0a9f9562 in OID 0x530 table. Same mapping works on Win10 3.4 and Win11 3.14

description: Schema 0x160 naming correction: 0x160 is used by Reparse Point Index (OIDs 0x540/0x541) via InitializeReparseIndexTable not by Security Descriptors (OID 0x530). OID 0x530 uses stream-type table without schema. Schema name 'Security Descriptor' is misleading.
static_analysis_notes: InitializeReparseIndexTable creates 0x540/0x541 with schema 0x160. OID 0x530 created by InitializeSecurityDescriptorStream separately
raw_analysis_notes: OID 0x540 B+ tree confirmed using schema 0x160 key structure (24-byte keys). OID 0x530 B+ tree uses different key structure (16-byte keys with hash-based lookup) | DOWNGRADED to INFERRED 2026-06-18 (all-disk re-verify: offset/value RD-confirmed, semantic label not disk-checkable — was CONFIRMED): Pure schema-naming correction; verified against structure_reference (0x530 schema='—', 0x540 schema=0x160). The InitializeReparseIndexTable attribution is E2/static — not byte-checkable. Confirmed consistent, label not independent

description: Reparse Index (OID 0x540/0x541 schema 0x160) key structure decoded (24 bytes): u32 marker 0x80000001 @0 (single-instance embedded-subrecord marker; the "key_type=0x0001/key_flags=0x8000" split is just a coincidental LE re-read of this u32) + ReparseTag(u32)@4 + _REFS_FILE_REFERENCE(16B)@8. The 16B file-ref is the file's 128-bit reference: word0 (entry ordinal/file-key, small 2..32) @8, containing-directory OID @0x10 (both from [fcb+0x58]+0xf8/+0x100). DISK-VERIFIED 2026-07-05: every index OID@0x10 matches a reparse file's PARENT directory OID (242/242 across 3 images: mftecmd 93/93, insider 42/42, specials 107/107) -- it is the containing directory's OID, NOT a per-file 'own' OID (ReFS files have none). Value: minimal/empty (pure existence index). ONE row per reparse-bearing OBJECT (FCB), inserted by RefsCreateReparsePointInternal via MsInsertRow(...,0x32), deleted by RefsDeleteReparsePointInternal/RefsRemoveReparsePoint via MsDeleteRow. NO tag filter -- ALL tags indexed (mount/symlink/WSL/etc); enumerate uses a match-ALL cursor.
static_analysis_notes: DRIVER-VERIFIED 2026-07-05 (E2): RefsCreateReparsePointInternal (win11 @388290, insider @498695) builds key = {0x80000001, param5=tag, [fcb+0x58]+0xf8, [fcb+0x58]+0x100}; RefsGetFirst/NextReparseInfo (win11 @457918/@458037) copy 3x u64 out (24B key); RefsPackFileId (@105663) proves _REFS_FILE_REFERENCE=16B (word0@0, OID@8); RefsIsSystemObjectId/RefsHasSystemName (@103061/@112811) test OID at struct+8. Prior "not verified in driver code" now resolved; ParentOID label corrected to file-ref word0.
raw_analysis_notes: Parsed on win11refs4gattributes.raw: 162 entries (161 SYMLINK + 1 LX_FIFO 0x80000024 - corrected from AF_UNIX, disk-verified + findings #289/E41). win10refs2g.raw: 3 SYMLINK. Key structure consistent across versions. Tool: refs_reparse.py. Per-OBJECT keying => hard-linked reparse NAMES collapse to fewer index rows; but a per-object collapse alone does NOT reconcile 444-names/330-rows on win11refstestmftecmd (18/85/327 groupings all miss 330 -- batch12 2026-07-05); the residual gap is the OLD reparse walker undercount (DEFECT1/C1: dropped non-resident len<=84 files), fixed in forefst C1.

description: WSL stores Linux metadata in Extended Attributes ($LXUID $LXGID $LXMOD $LXDEV) using FILE_FULL_EA_INFORMATION format NOT in reparse data. WSL special files use reparse tags AF_UNIX 0x80000023, LX_FIFO 0x80000024, LX_CHR 0x80000025, LX_BLK 0x80000026 (CORRECTED off-by-one, E41/#341; confirmed on win11refs2gtargeted). $LXDEV = 8 bytes (u32 major + u32 minor); $LXUID/$LXGID/$LXMOD = 4B. WSL symlinks use IO_REPARSE_TAG_LX_SYMLINK (0xA000001D). Requires -o metadata mount option.
static_analysis_notes: WSL EA format documented in refs_attributes.py. Windows FILE_FULL_EA_INFORMATION is standard Windows EA chain format
raw_analysis_notes: EA parsing verified on win11refs4gattributes.raw WSL files. $LXUID/$LXGID/$LXMOD extracted. See ra_step4_3_attributes_report.md and ra_step4_14_reparse_points_report.md

description: SUPB page has NO self-checksum (offset 0x08 always 0x00000000). SUPB is outside the Merkle-tree checksum chain — it is the root and has no parent to store its checksum. Silently repaired by driver on v3.14 mount.
static_analysis_notes: On-disk layout observation; not directly verifiable from driver code
raw_analysis_notes: Verified on win11refsmini_beforecorruption.raw and post-mount image: SUPB offset 0x08 = 0 in both. See ra_step4_15_corrupted_metadata_report.md

description: SUPB corruption is silently repaired during mount by Win11 3.14 driver. Corrupted Volume GUID byte (0x2A→0xD5) at SUPB+0x55 was restored to original value during mount. Repair occurs as part of normal checkpoint update cycle (CoW write of fresh SUPB).
static_analysis_notes: On-disk layout observation; not directly verifiable from driver code
raw_analysis_notes: Binary comparison: corrupted byte 0xD5 reverted to 0x2A after mount. 82 pages changed during mount. See ra_step4_15_corrupted_metadata_report.md

description: SUPB Volume GUID at offset 0x50 (16 bytes) is non-critical for bootstrap. Corrupting the GUID does not prevent mount because the driver locates checkpoints via fixed-offset CHKP LCN pointers at SUPB+0xC0/0xC8 not via the GUID.

description: Deep container row structure: 160-byte (4K/CRC64) with 13 decoded fields. 224-byte (64K or SHA256) with 128-byte extended area. Universal parsing: CPC at +0x18, CSC at value[len-16:len-8]. Container flags bitmask decoded. Extended area (0x50+) = per-container integrity checksum buffer managed by CmsIntegrityState. Size = num_slots x 16: 4 slots (64B) for CRC64, 8 slots (128B) for SHA256. E2: GetNumAllocatedForContainerEntry + PersistContainerCacheWorker confirm via field_18c. E1: zero on 80+ images except 1 container on win10refs2tspecials. Ref: report_container_and_allocator_tables.md §1.3.1

raw_analysis_notes: Extended area decoded as integrity checksum buffer (2026-05-23)

description: Deep allocator row structure: key=[range_start,range_length]. Two value formats: bitmap (24B header + 2048B bitmap; 1=allocated 0=free; free_count=range_len-bits_set; field_22=used_count) and compact (24B; flags=0x2 for fully allocated). Three-tier: Small (real LCN root #12), Medium (virtual root #1), Container (virtual root #2)

description: Deep row structure: Key=[StartLCN(u64), BlockCount(u64)]. Value=28B header + BlockCount*u16 refcount array + 4B trailing (2080B for bc=0x400). Header: StartLCN(redundant), BlockCount(redundant), ModificationStamp(u64), TotalRefCount(u32). Array entry bitfield: RefCount(13:0), Flag14(14), Shared(15). TotalRefCount=sum(entry&0x3FFF). Dedup flags 0x8000/0x4000 on dedup volumes. Table exists from v3.4 (empty) but populated only on v3.14+. 1305 rows across 83 images; 1302/1302 sum verification matches

description: Deep row structure: Key=32B [u64 zero, u64 ParentOID, u64 zero, u64 ChildOID]. Value overlaps key (key_offset==val_offset key_length==val_length). Pure set/index with no payload data; 48-byte rows. Only directories tracked; no multi-parent entries across 31392 entries on 76 images. Schema 0xe040 u32[7]=0x08 = key-comparison-rules selector (8 -> CmsRulesPARENT_CHILD_LINK; NOT a bitfield, E50); value-overlaps-key is a caller-side same-buffer construction (AddParentChildLink), not a schema flag. Format version-invariant v3.4-v3.14
static_analysis_notes: E2 2026-06-19: u32[7] (schema-def +0x1C) = KEY-COMPARISON-RULES selector (enum 1..0x14), NOT a bitfield. RegisterSchema win11 @83852 bounds-checks <0x15 and indexes key-rules table DAT_1401cb370 (GetKeyRulesInternal @102170); 0xe040 -> 8 -> CmsRulesPARENT_CHILD_LINK::CompareKeys; .rdata system-schema table reproduces disk values exactly. value-overlaps-key is caller-side: AddParentChildLink @232562 passes the SAME 32-byte buffer as key+value; InitializeIndexEntry @8710 detects value_ptr<=key_ptr<=value_ptr+len and sets value_offset=key_offset. No schema-flag/bit-0x08 test.
raw_analysis_notes: See ra_step4_25_parent_child_table_report.md | CORRECTED 2026-06-19 (E50): u32[7]=0x08 confirmed unique to 0xe040; value-overlaps-key 238/238 PCT rows, 0 on all other tables. Bit-3 hypothesis REFUTED on disk: schema 0x130 (u32[7]=0x9, bit3 set) tables do NOT overlap. u32[7] is a key-rules enum, not a bitfield. See pct_schema_u32_7_2026-06-19.md.

description: OID monotonic assignment: user OIDs start at 0x700 (hardcoded in MsSetMinimumNewObjectId) and increment sequentially via atomic GenerateIdentifier. OIDs are NEVER reused after deletion (DeleteIdentifier never decrements counter). Gaps = deleted objects. Confirmed on 66+ images and by static analysis across 3 driver versions (128 OID functions decompiled, see FS_OTBL_SA_001-010).

description: OID chronology analysis: 400 objects in range 0x700-0x8F9 on test image (76.6% density). 128-bit File IDs encode table OID (upper 8B) + sequential entry index (lower 8B). Generation counter at Object Table value offset 0x18 = checkpoint virtual clock at creation/last update. ReFS provides stronger chronological guarantees than NTFS (no record reuse)

description: CmsObjectTable::InitializeTable: bootstraps OID counter at mount time. Schema 0xe030. 815 bytes (Insider).

description: MsInitializeWellKnownObjectId: trivial 12-byte helper constructing 128-bit SmsBigIdentifier from a 64-bit well-known OID value (e.g. 0x600=root dir, 0x500=volume info). Sets upper 8 bytes to 0.
static_analysis_notes: ObjectIdLifecycle.md §2.6
raw_analysis_notes: See ObjectIdLifecycle.md

description: MsGetObjectRecordPayload: 46-byte helper extracting LcnWithChecksum pointer from Object Table row. Returns pointer to the page reference within the row's value buffer at offset derived from row header.
static_analysis_notes: ObjectIdLifecycle.md §3.2
raw_analysis_notes: See ObjectIdLifecycle.md

description: RefsOpenFcbById: opens a file by its 128-bit OID. Largest Refs-layer OID consumer (5792B Win11 / 5958B Insider). Call chain: OID lookup → FCB create → RefsMapStandardInfo → security load → RefsOpenFile. Two FCB creation paths (normal + hardlink/special).
static_analysis_notes: RefsOpenFcbById.md. Win11 takes explicit _IRP*; Insider uses opaque param (+166 bytes for attestation checks)
raw_analysis_notes: See RefsOpenFcbById.md

description: CmsObjectTable::DeleteIdentifier: removes OID from Object Table B+-tree. DOES NOT decrement counter — guarantees OIDs are never reused after deletion. 30 bytes (Insider, thin wrapper around DeleteRow).
static_analysis_notes: ObjectIdLifecycle.md §4.1. Code-level proof: no counter decrement in any version. Only calls DeleteRow on the B+-tree
raw_analysis_notes: See ObjectIdLifecycle.md | DOWNGRADED to INFERRED 2026-06-18 (all-disk re-verify: offset/value RD-confirmed, semantic label not disk-checkable — was CONFIRMED): The observable consequence (no OID reuse, gaps=deletions) is CONFIRMED-ALLDISK via RA_006/RA_007; the function-internal 'no decrement' assertion is INFERRED from those plus E2 static.

description: CmsObjectTable::InsertIntoParentChildTable: inserts [parent_OID child_OID] pair into Parent-Child Table (schema 0xe040). Called for every directory entry creation. Enables reverse lookup (find children of a parent). ~280 bytes (Insider).
static_analysis_notes: ObjectIdLifecycle.md §5.1. Same insert pattern across all versions. Key format: [0 parent_OID 0 child_OID]
raw_analysis_notes: See ObjectIdLifecycle.md

description: DriverEntry initialization: registers IRP dispatch table (IRP_MJ_CREATE/CLEANUP/CLOSE/READ/WRITE/DEVICE_CONTROL/SET_INFORMATION/QUERY_INFORMATION/etc.). Initializes global state and registry configuration. ~7533 bytes Win10.
static_analysis_notes: See DriverEntry.md. PDB confirms all IRP handler registrations

description: RefsIsBootSectorOurs: validates boot sector ownership. Checks "ReFS" signature at +0x03, sector size power-of-2, volume sector count > 0. Returns BOOLEAN.
static_analysis_notes: See RefsIsBootSectorOurs.md. Decompiled from Insider build

description: RefsCommonCreate: core file/directory create and open logic (~18679/19580 bytes). Calls RefsEditFileBegin which assigns unique OID from Object Table during file creation.

description: RefsComputeStandardInformationFromFcb (Win11 only): constructs complete 124-byte $STANDARD_INFORMATION from FCB fields. Not present in Win10.

description: Snapshot extent entries use same 24-byte format (VLCN+flags+file_VCN+run_length) as regular type-0x40 data runs. Unified extent storage across non-resident directory entries and embedded DATA entries. Confirmed on 21 snapshots across 4 images.

description: Address translation shift = CPC.bit_length() = 15 (4K) / 11 (64K), NOT log2(CPC) = 14/10. Driver stores log2(CPC) at volume+0x50 and adds 1 before shifting (GetContainerIdFromRealRange). Errata E18.

raw_analysis_notes: VLCN 81408 with shift=15 resolves to CID 2 (MSB+ valid); shift=14 gives CID 4 (wrong page). Verified on 20 images

description: Production v3.14 has 29 schemas (13 system + 16 attribute), not 30 (14+16). 14 system schemas is the Insider count (adds 0xe140 Vol Attestation). Errata E14.
static_analysis_notes: schema tool output on 20 images confirms 13/16 split for v3.14 and 14/16 for Insider
raw_analysis_notes: All 14 v3.14 test images show 29 schemas; Insider shows 30

description: key_flags is u16 at key+0x02, NOT first 4 bytes of row header. Values 0x68/0x2a8 are row sizes. CORRECTION: key_flags take ONLY {0x01=resident, 0x02=non-resident}; 0x04 NEVER appears. Directories are key_flags 0x02 with the directory attribute bit 0x10000000 set in the value attribute word (value+0x40), NOT a separate 0x04 flag. Distribution 111 images/525K entries: {0x01: 409483 ; 0x02: 115682 ; 0x04: 0}. Errata E15/E17 "0x04=directory" was wrong.
static_analysis_notes: forefst.py reads key type at kd[0:2] and filename at kd[4:]. No code path writes key_flags=0x04; RefsAddFileNameIndexEntry ORs dir bit 0x10000000 into the attribute word.
raw_analysis_notes: key_flags distribution {0x01:409483, 0x02:115682, 0x04:0} across 111 images. Directories: kf=0x02 + attrs bit 0x10000000 at value+0x40 (e.g. $RECYCLE.BIN kf=0x02 attrs=0x10000006).

description: Directory key has no Reserved(4) field at 0x04. Correct: Type(2)+key_flags(2)+Filename(var) starting at offset 0x04. Errata E16.
static_analysis_notes: forefst.py kd[4:].decode(utf-16-le) produces correct filenames
raw_analysis_notes: Row size proof: coucou=6 chars -> key=4+12=16B; with Reserved(4) it would be 20B giving 108 not 104=0x68

description: VBR format-time fields (0x2A checksum algo, 0x2C volume flags, 0x48 format GUID) are NEVER modified during version upgrade. Upgraded volumes retain original VBR values while CHKP shows new version.

static_analysis_notes: verify_static.py: decompiled code / function catalog evidence

description: E20 RETRACTED: ADS are always resident (inline). 956 ADS across 46 images all have StreamSummary flags=0. 29 snapshot entries have SS flags=2. 28 entries initially misclassified as extent-based ADS were actually snapshots (same 0x000500B0 descriptor). Discriminator: StreamSummary flags (u16) at val[0x10] — 0=ADS, 2=snapshot.
static_analysis_notes: RefsConvertToNonResident accepts type 0xB0; the 2KB threshold IS reached by a large ADS (>=2KB) which becomes non-resident/extent-backed (type-0x0 record; E61). ADS-always-inline holds only <2KB; the val[0x10] 0=ADS/2=snapshot discriminator is unaffected

description: E21: $SI field corrections — ReparseTag at +0x54 (not 0x4C), HardLinkCount at +0x70 (not 0x48), +0x48 is conditional u64, +0x50 is u16-derived u32. Five corrections from RefsComputeStandardInformationFromFcb decompilation.
static_analysis_notes: RefsComputeStandardInformationFromFcb: *(u32*)(SI+0x54) = *(u32*)(FCB+0x9c) (ReparseTag), *(u32*)(SI+0x70) from RefsConvertToStandardInfoLinkCount
raw_analysis_notes: 525K entries / 111 images: +0x54 correct for symlinks. +0x70 always 0 or 1. CORRECTION: the prior "confirmed cross-directory hard links" (87/41 multi-parent OIDs) were a parser artifact — non-resident type-0x30 value[0x08] is the home-directory OID back-reference (shared by siblings), not a child OID. $SI+0x70 is the resident-layout HLC (always 0/1) and is NOT the multi-name counter. The pre-step6 corpus had no genuine hard links, but they DO exist (non-resident type-0x30 mechanism) and were since observed on win11refs2gtargeted (FN_LINK_002/#340)

description: BRC key = start_lcn(u64) + block_count(u64 always 0x400). Value = 1024 per-cluster u16: bit 15=shared/dedup-managed, bit 14=dedup metadata flag, bits 13:0=reference count (0-505 observed). Value header at +0x18 = TotalRefCount (u32) == Sum(array entry & 0x3FFF), upper 16 bits always 0 (230/230 rows, master B.6).
static_analysis_notes: BRC table accessed via root #6; key/value structure confirmed in decompilation
raw_analysis_notes: CORRECTED 2026-06-19 (master B.6): +0x18 = TotalRefCount = Sum(entry&0x3FFF), re-confirmed 230/230 rows 2026-06-17. The earlier 'compound field / 67/97 violations / not simple total' reading was a 2026-06-16 citation error, retracted. | 97 BRC rows on win11refs8gdedup.raw; 99328 tracked clusters; val+0x18 has 67/97 violations as simple sum

description: MLog opcode-to-operation sequences: CREATE=INSERT+INSERT+SET_OBJREC (99.9%), DELETE=DELETE/DEL_TABLE chain, RENAME=DELETE+DELETE+INSERT+INSERT, MOVE=DELETE+REPARENT+INSERT. 15 opcodes observed on v3.14 (of 37), 12 on v3.4.

VBR is format-time only — upgrade never rewrites it. 4 zero-GUID v3.14 images identifiable by chksum_algo=0x0000 (pre-v3.14 format).

description: Type-0x10 $SI+0x38 (DataSize slot) = 0 across all versions / 110 images (CONFIRMED). The value previously attributed to 'resident directory-entry $SI DataSize' is the type-0x30 INDEX-ENTRY FileSize at value+0x58 (master C.2) — a DIFFERENT structure, not $SI+0x38.

Type-0x10 $SI+0x38 itself is 0. | 110 images / 525145 entries: ALL child OID type 0x10 $SI have DataSize=0. Resident directory entries: 32573/33263 exact match (97.9%). FileSize == embedded $DATA content size (8-byte aligned; FileSize >= content always).

description: Directory DataSize=0 invariant: 32490/32490 directories (100%) have $SI+0x38 = 0 across all 110 tested images and all versions (v3.4 through v3.14+/insider). Directories never have a $DATA stream, so DataSize is always zero.

description: User OIDs (>=0x600) have exactly 4 B+-tree key types: 0x10 ($SI), 0x20 (reverse index), 0x30 (filenames), 0x40 (extents). Types 0x80-0x100 are NEVER top-level keys. Verified across 32721 user OIDs on 110 images.

description: Type 0x10 value contains embedded B+-tree with $OBJ_LINK (0x39/0x38) + $I30_INDEX (0x90) sub-records. 99.4% have exactly 2 rows. 0.3% have 3 (adds $REPARSE). <0.01% have 4 (adds $EA).

raw_analysis_notes: 110 images / 32721 entries: 32510 have 2 rows (99.4%), 121 have 1 row, 86 have 3 rows, 4 have 4 rows.

description: $OBJ_LINK (type 0x39 on v3.7+, 0x38 on v3.4) stores primary filename + parent OID within each object's type 0x10 B+-tree record. Enables path reconstruction from Object Table alone without directory walk. Driver: RefsInitializeObjLinkRow.

raw_analysis_notes: 110 images: v3.4 images use 0x38 (509 entries), v3.7+ use 0x39 (32077 MI + 14 SI). Parent OID verified via directory walk cross-reference.

description: $OBJ_LINK version evolution: type 0x38 (v3.4, no instance markers) → type 0x39 (v3.7+, with 0x80000002 MI marker). Upgraded volumes retain v3.4 format for existing entries. Version check at VCB+0x2ACC.
static_analysis_notes: Version check in decompilation: if VCB+0x2ACC == 0x38 use legacy format
raw_analysis_notes: win10to11refs4g.raw (v3.4→v3.14 upgrade): 39 OIDs all retain type 0x38 despite v3.14 version number.

description: Embedded B+-tree key/value overlap: key[0:8] = le64(value_length), key[8:] = value[0:]. Row offset table at end of value: pairs of [le16 offset][0xFFFF]. Same format used in type 0x10 and resident type 0x30 values.
raw_analysis_notes: Cross-verified across 32721 type 0x10 entries: key[0:8] as le64 matches value length in 100% of parsed rows.

description: Resident type 0x30 sub-record distribution (71162 entries across 110 images): MI_0x80=75303, SI_0x80=30360, SI_0xD0=3879, SI_0xE0=3854, SI_0xC0=2001, MI_0xB0=979, MI_0x100=7. Types 0x80 ($DATA), 0xB0 ($SNAPSHOT), 0xC0 ($REPARSE), 0xD0 ($EA_INFO), 0xE0 ($EA), 0x100 ($EFS) confirmed as embedded sub-records.
raw_analysis_notes: 110 images scanned with parse_resident_btree_rows. Sub-record types match F.2 schema table attribute codes exactly.

description: $OBJ_LINK value format (v3.7+): +0x00=marker(4B) +0x04=type(2B) +0x06=subtype(2B) +0x08=parent_oid(le32) +0x0C=reserved(12B zeros) +0x18=filename(UTF-16LE). Total varies with filename length (26-82 bytes observed).
raw_analysis_notes: 32077 entries parsed: parent_oid at +0x08 matches directory walk parent. Filename at +0x18 matches type 0x30 key. Reserved bytes +0x0C always zero.

description: InternalFlags ($SI+0x24) transition: zero on ALL v3.4/v3.7/v3.9/v3.10 images (0/18). Non-zero on ALL v3.14 images (90/90, 368380 entries). Transition at v3.14, not v3.10 as E25 conservative estimate.

raw_analysis_notes: 111 images / 525165 entries: zero on v3.4-v3.10 (8389 entries), non-zero on v3.14 (368380). Bits 4-5 via callers.

description: InternalFlags bit mapping: bit0=DELETE/DISPOSITION (FCB bit27 — set in DeleteDirectoryOnDisk/EFS paths, NOT integrity; integrity-stream = file_attrs 0x8000 per MD_INTG_RA_001/#342), bit1=DEDUP/COW(FCB bit22), bit2=BIT_0xB(FCB bit11), bit3=BIT_0x1F(FCB bit31). Most common: 0x08 (BIT_0x1F at 33553 on Insider). Bits 4-5 from RefsConvertToStandardInfoLinkCount.
static_analysis_notes: Decompiled at 1400b7454 (Win11) / 1400b0604 (Insider). Identical logic.

description: ExternalFileId_2/3 populated ONLY for cross-volume operations. RefsConvertToStandardInfoExternalId compares source volume with current; zeros if same. ExtFid3=source parent OID (0x700+ range).

raw_analysis_notes: 603/525165 non-zero ExtFid_2 entries. All on v3.4 images or upgraded. Zero on all native v3.14.

description: $SI+0x58 (v3.4 "ExternalFileId_1" / v3.7+ "VersionRefCount") = NextFileId, a directory CHILD-CREATION ordinal — NOT a version/write counter. Write path RefsMoveFile increments the parent dir SCB's NextFileId (SCB+0x1b8 win11/+0x1a8 insider, init=1) and stamps the new child FCB+0x158. Non-zero on 100% of v3.4 entries (3601/3601), values small integers = assigned child ordinals.
static_analysis_notes: RefsMoveFile increments parent SCB NextFileId (L192932) + RefsPersistNextFileId; persisted to dir own-row only when version<0x30b (else object-record payload). Read in RefsComputeStandardInformationFromFcb from dir SCB.
raw_analysis_notes: 3601/3601 v3.4 entries non-zero; values = child ordinals (near-contiguous from 2). dir own-row = max(child ordinal): 0 violations across 232 dirs on v3.7/3.9/3.10.

description: HardLinkCount ($SI+0x70) >1: ZERO across 525165 entries / 111 images because $SI+0x70 is a RESIDENT-LAYOUT field always 0/1 — it is NOT the multi-name counter. Genuine hard links use the non-resident type-0x30 mechanism (FN_LINK_002/#340), observed on win11refs2gtargeted. Values: 0 (1004 special entries) or 1 (524161 regular entries).
raw_analysis_notes: 111 images, 525165 entries: 0 with HLC>1. Distribution: 0=1004, 1=524161.

description: NextFileId ($SI+0x58, formerly "VersionRefCount") upper 32 always 0 across 521564 v3.7+ entries; full u64, max observed 110001 (a child ordinal, NOT a version/write count). 3-population model: resident-file inline $SI always populated (all versions incl native v3.14); dir/file own-rows populated v3.4-v3.10 but 0 on native v3.14 (version<0x30b persist gate).

raw_analysis_notes: 521564 v3.7+ entries, hi=0 on 100%. Max=110001. Resident files populated on native v3.14; dir/file own-rows 0 on native v3.14.

The byte layout (summary 0x30, content@0x3C, size@0x20) is CONFIRMED on v3.14

description: $SNAPSHOT/ADS (0xB0): identical stream summary format to SI $DATA. Storage type at val[0x10]: 0=ADS, non-zero=snapshot. 979 MI entries across 110 images (678 ADS + 30 snapshots).

description: $REPARSE_V2 (0xC0): REPARSE_DATA_BUFFER stored verbatim at val[0x0C]. Tag at val[0x0C], data_length at val[0x10]. CORRECTION: 0x8000001B = APPEXECLINK (app execution alias), the tag observed on winsider — NOT WCI/WOF. The prior _REPARSE_TAGS table was broadly mislabeled (off-by-one), now corrected to authoritative ntifs.h values: 0x80000024=LX_FIFO not AF_UNIX; AF_UNIX=0x80000023; WOF=0x80000017; WCI=0x80000018. WOF/WCI/DEDUP (0x80000013) NOT observed; AF_UNIX (0x80000023), LX_CHR (0x80000025), LX_BLK (0x80000026) NOW OBSERVED on win11refs2gtargeted (FS_REPS_RA_003/#341). Inventory (2084 instances / 47 images): SYMLINK 0xA000000C=2007, MOUNT_POINT 0xA0000003=47, APPEXECLINK 0x8000001B=28, LX_FIFO 0x80000024=2 (empty, the lxfifo file, mode S_IFIFO). refs.sys structurally recognizes only symlink/junction; all other tags stored verbatim.
static_analysis_notes: RefsSetReparsePointInternal handles only 0xa000000c/0xa0000003; all other tags pass through. No driver hits for WOF/WCI/dedup constants (minifilter-handled).
raw_analysis_notes: APPEXECLINK=0x8000001B (28 on winsider), not WCI/WOF. LX_FIFO=0x80000024 (the lxfifo file). WOF (0x80000017) + WCI (0x80000018) + DEDUP NOT observed; AF_UNIX 0x80000023 + LX_CHR 0x80000025 + LX_BLK 0x80000026 NOW OBSERVED on win11refs2gtargeted (#341); LX_SYMLINK 0xA000001D NOW OBSERVED on win11refs8g (step6, E57). refsanalysis _REPARSE_TAGS table rebuilt to authoritative values (maintainer flag).

description: $EA_INFO (0xD0): fixed 20B. CORRECTION: $SI+0x50 PackedEaSize (v3.10+) = val[0x0C] (= Σ(5+EaNameLength+EaValueLength), the NTFS-conventional PackedEaSize cached in FCB+0xA0), NOT val[0x10]. val[0x10] = Σ align4(8+nameLen+1+valLen) = serialized on-disk footprint. The two size fields were inverted in the prior claim. 3889 entries across 3 v3.14 images.

description: $SI+0x40 ClassTag = the file LastUsn (byte offset of its most recent $UsnJrnl:$J record, from journal write cursor VCB+0x3a0 / FCB+0xe0; 8-byte aligned). $SI+0x48 SessionTimestamp = the USN JOURNAL ID (VCB+0x388, a FILETIME). NOT a storage-tiering tag (the E3/E24 tiering theory is DISPROVEN). FCB bit23 = cached last-USN valid for current journal epoch. Both written atomically by RefsWriteFcbUsnRecordToJournal->RefsPartialSetStandardInfo (gated VCB+0x18 & 0x80000), explaining the 0-violation co-occurrence.
static_analysis_notes: RefsWriteFcbUsnRecordToJournal->RefsPartialSetStandardInfo(...,0x40,0x10,...) gated VCB+0x18 & 0x80000 (win11 L446230). Journal id VCB+0x388 set at init (L159523/L445682). Read code present since v3.4 (VCB+0x360, FCB+4 bit).
raw_analysis_notes: 717/717 ClassTag values 8-byte aligned (USN records are 8-aligned); exactly 1 distinct SessionTimestamp per volume (journal id, not per-file); upper-32 always 0; nonzero only on v3.14+ images with active USN journal.

description: InternalFlags ($SI+0x24) bit5 (0x20) = symlink/junction REDIRECTION-TRUST level (FCB[0xf4] bit1 from IoComputeRedirectionTrustLevel), NOT "HardLinkCount drops to 0". Written only in RefsSetReparsePointInternal for tags 0xa000000c/0xa0000003. The HLC-zero path (RefsConvertToStandardInfoLinkCount) ORs bit2 (0x04), never 0x20.
static_analysis_notes: RefsSetReparsePointInternal sets FCB[0xf4] (L174306); RefsComputeStandardInformationFromFcb shifts FCB[0xf4] bits 0-1 into $SI+0x24 bits 4-5 (L159547). Link-count-zero path ORs param_4=0x04 (insider L511461).
raw_analysis_notes: winsider file_own internal_flags=0x20 count = 78 = EXACTLY the 78 reparse points (46 junctions + 32 symlinks). Across 47 v3.14+ images with bit5 set, all 47 have a symlink/junction; 0 counterexamples. | DOWNGRADED to INFERRED 2026-06-18 (all-disk re-verify: offset/value RD-confirmed, semantic label not disk-checkable — was CONFIRMED): The 'redirection-trust level (FCB[0xf4] bit1 from IoComputeRedirectionTrustLevel)' semantic is static/E2-only. Disk confirms the 78=78 reparse-point correlation but the bit's exact FCB source is not disk-derivable.

description: RETRACTED: $SI+0x30 is NOT a USN/change-journal byte-offset. Type-0x10 $SI+0x30 = 0 on 0/32,629 own-rows across the corpus. The real per-file->journal link is LastUsn ($SI+0x40 / value+0x68) = virtual byte offset of the file's latest $UsnJrnl:$J record + UsnJournalId ($SI+0x48 / value+0x70).
static_analysis_notes: RefsComputeStandardInformationFromFcb: USN <- FCB+0x90 (L159515); on refresh FCB+0x90 overwritten from $DATA stream summary +0x10 (L465168). RefsUsnAppendData advances the write pointer by record length.

Proven: USN = LastUsn@value+0x68 (480/480 $J-record matches). | 0% nonzero on file_own + dir_own across ALL versions; file_resident 53-99.5% (tracks journal activity).

description: Type 0xF0 = USN change-journal $Max metadata (NTFS $UsnJrnl:$Max: MaximumSize@+0x00 / AllocationDelta@+0x08 / UsnId FILETIME@+0x10 / LowestValidUsn@+0x18). Unnamed single-instance attr (marker 0x80000001), 0x20-byte payload + 12B wrapper = 44B, on the "Change Journal" file under OID 0x520. Created by RefsSetupUsnJournal — present since v3.4. Distinct from 0x100/$EFS.
static_analysis_notes: RefsSetupUsnJournal: win11 CreateAttribute(0xf0,NULL,&body,0x20) L445618 / win10 RefsCreateAttributeWithValue(0xf0,NULL,0x20) L325274; READ LookupAttribute(0xf0,NULL). EFS path uses type 0x100.

description: E20 reaffirmed: ALL alternate data streams (ADS, type 0xB0) are inline/resident — every 0xB0 ADS has stream_flags=0x02 (159/159), including the single attr_flags=0x1000 ADS (still inline). The "genuine non-resident ADS" claim is REFUTED; attr-bit 0x1000 marks ADS-on-a-file-with-snapshots/stream-set, not non-residency.
static_analysis_notes: RefsConvertToNonResident accepts type 0xB0; a large ADS (>=2KB) DOES reach the 2KB threshold and becomes extent-backed (E61); ADS-always-inline holds only for content <2KB.
raw_analysis_notes: All 159 small ADS stream_flags=0x02 (inline). E20 (ADS always inline) HOLDS for ADS <2KB; a >=2KB ADS is extent-backed via a type-0x0 record (E61 - byte-exact on a 256B-2MB sweep).

description: MLog FOUR-layer model (resolves B2b): each data page = a LogCore data record. L1 = 0x78 (120)-byte record header (sig@0x00 + per-volume-magic@0x04 NOT-CRC + ver=1@0x08 + log-block-size-0x1000@0x0C + UUID@0x10 + counter@0x20 + LSN@0x28 + prevLSN@0x30 + length-in-4K-blocks@0x38/0x3C + entry-offset@0x54=0x78). L2 = entry header @record+0x78 (LSN@+0x00 + checksum@+0x08 + payload-len@+0x20 + payload-offset@+0x28 + type@+0x30=2-data/1-control). L3 = redo block _SmsRedoHeader @record+0xB0. L4 = _SmsRedoRecord entries.
static_analysis_notes: LogCoreWriteDataRecord + LogCoreScanDataRecord + LogValidateEntryHeader + LogInitializeEntryHeader; offsets identical in win10 (v3.4) win11 (v3.14) and insider. Entry payload-offset value is version-dependent: 0x38 (v3.4-v3.14) vs 0x40 (Insider; entry header 56->64B; redo block 0xB0->0xB8).
raw_analysis_notes: probe_logcore_record.py PASS on 10 images (v3.4/3.7/3.14/upgraded/compression/262K-record/Insider-4K+64K). 0x0C stays 0x1000 on a 64K-cluster volume (proves log-block-size != cluster-size). LSN chains prevLSN[n]==LSN[n-1] pre circular-buffer-wrap.

description: Hard-link mechanism (v3.14): each name = a separate type-0x30 non-resident dir entry (key_flags 0x02, 84B value). value+0x00 = per-directory child ordinal (= NextFileId $SI+0x58), shared by one file's names but NOT a globally-unique FileId (reused per directory, collides across sibling dirs under a shared home). value+0x08 = home-dir backref OID (the directory the file was first created in, #327). No explicit on-disk HardLinkCount; link count = number of names resolving to the SAME content-bearing type-0x40 record (owner-dir OID, file_id) -- local (parent,file_id) if alloc>0 else home backref to (home,file_id) through any alloc=0 stub; the (home,ordinal,size,created,modified) tuple over-merges AND over-splits and is NOT used (content-aware re-fix 2026-06-19, MD_DATA_RA_006/master J). $SI+0x70 'HardLinkCount' is a resident-layout field, always 1. Supersedes the retracted '110 true hard links' artifact (#292/E33).
static_analysis_notes: win11_4b0558f6 (v3.14 RTM): RefsComputeStandardInformationFromFcb ($SI+0x70<-FCB+0xb4 @L159550, $SI+0x58<-SCB+0x1b8 @L159529); RefsConvertToStandardInfoLinkCount writes offset 0x70 size 4; RefsLinkFileToSelf/RefsAddLink copy the home pair under tag 0x80000040. All four absent in win10 v3.4.
raw_analysis_notes: win11refs2gtargeted.raw: fsutil oracle file1=4 names, file2=2, survivor=1; on disk (raw struct.unpack) file1's 4 entries all ordinal 3, file2's 2 all ordinal 4, survivor 5, all home backref 0x600; ordinal 3 reused across dir1(0x705)+dir2(0x706) proving per-directory (not unique). Grouping by (home,ordinal,size,ctime,mtime) reproduces fsutil hardlink list exactly.

description: WSL special-file reparse tags confirmed on disk (win11refs2gtargeted): AF_UNIX 0x80000023, LX_FIFO 0x80000024, LX_CHR 0x80000025, LX_BLK 0x80000026. LX_SYMLINK 0xA000001D NOW OBSERVED on win11refs8g (step6, 2026-07-03): produced by 'ln -s /etc/passwd' (absolute Linux target) and 'cp -a' of an ext4 symlink; 'ln -s targetfile.txt' (relative, Windows-resolvable) still yields a Windows SYMLINK 0xA000000C (E57). Linux metadata in EAs: $LXUID/$LXGID/$LXMOD = u32 (4B); $LXDEV = 8 bytes = u32 major + u32 minor, present only on device nodes (chr/blk). Reparse tag VALUES are minifilter-handled (opaque to refs.sys); the $LX* EAs ARE parsed by refs.sys. Corrects the prior off-by-one tag guesses and '$LXDEV=4B / not observed'.
static_analysis_notes: Tags 0x80000023-26: 0 matches in all builds (win10/win11/insider) -> WslFs/lxcore minifilter, not refs.sys. $LX* EAs parsed by RefsQueryLxMetadataEa (win11_4b0558f6 @14025ae3c; insider @14029bd48): validates $LXDEV EaValueLength==8 and splits major@+0x58/minor@+0x5c; absent in win10 v3.4.
raw_analysis_notes: win11refs2gtargeted.raw: 5 WSL files; tags read by struct.unpack match exactly; $LXDEV testchr=1,3 testblk=7,0 (matches 'mknod c 1 3'/'b 7 0' oracle); $LXDEV present only on chr/blk. On win11refs2gtargeted the lxsymlink tag was 0xA000000C (relative target); LX_SYMLINK 0xA000001D IS produced on win11refs8g (step6) via 'ln -s /etc/passwd' (m2, /etc/passwd) and 'cp -a' ext4 symlink (m3, /etc/hostname) — raw buffer 1d0000a0..02000000+UTF-8 target, target at buffer+0x0C, datalen=4+len (E57).

description: Integrity-stream marker on a file = file_attrs bit 0x8000 (FILE_ATTRIBUTE_INTEGRITY_STREAM) ONLY. $SI+0x24 internal_flags has NO integrity-specific bit: its bit0 (0x01) = FCB+0x08 bit 27, a delete-disposition/EFS transient-state flag (set in DeleteDirectoryOnDisk), NOT integrity. Corrects the prior 'bit0=INTEGRITY(FCB bit 27)' label (E-integrity-bit / supersedes the gloss in MD_SI_RA_006).
static_analysis_notes: win11_4b0558f6 (+insider): RefsSetIntegrity @1402840a8 sets/clears SCB+0x98 bit 0x8000 (|=0x8000 / &=0xffff7fff @L422441-422449) -> reflected to $SI+0x20. RefsComputeStandardInformationInternalFromFcb @1400b7454 builds $SI+0x24 from FCB+0x08 bits only (bit0<-bit27), never SCB+0x98/0x8000. FCB bit 27 set in DeleteDirectoryOnDisk and consulted in EfsDecryptFile.
raw_analysis_notes: 4 v3.14 integrity images: file_attrs 0x8000 = 100% correlation with Get-FileIntegrity Enabled; win11refs2g_setintegritystreams1 has 5953 integrity files, 0 with $SI+0x24 bit0 set. integritytest.txt: attrs 0x00008020 (0x8000 set), internal_flags 0x08 (bit0 clear).

description: MLog control-page field at page+0x04 is a per-volume constant magic, NOT a CRC. Confirmed by reformat differential on the same partition: win11refs2gtargeted +0x04 = 0x6d790647; its reformatted copy = 0x5e524606, while ~90% of the control-page bytes are identical (the volume serial/GUID also changed) — a content CRC would match across the identical bytes, a per-volume constant differs. Corroborates E42/#329/#325b.
static_analysis_notes: win11_4b0558f6: LogCoreWriteDataRecord writes record+0x04 by copy from handle+0x2fe8 (L62408); LogValidateEntryHeader validates by EQUALITY not checksum (L134436); real per-record checksum is the separate LogVerifyChecksumEntryHeader (XOR-fold @ entry+0x08 = page+0x80). Same mechanism in win10 v3.4 (handle 0x2ecc/0x2ed0) and insider (0x2fe4/0x2fe8).
raw_analysis_notes: win11refs2gtargeted.raw vs _reformated.raw control pages @0x9000000: +0x04 = 0x6d790647 vs 0x5e524606; all 195 MLog records share the same +0x04 while page+0x80 XOR-fold takes 193 distinct values -> +0x04 is a stored per-volume constant, not a CRC. (Per-volume value; earlier docs quoted 0x6d6c6f07/0x5e534d06 from a pre-regeneration image.)

description: CoW checkpoint-comparison limitation RD-verified across the corpus: the two CHKP copies ALWAYS decode to identical 13-root pointer lists — confirmed on clean (win11refsmini), corrupted (win11refsmini_aftercorruption + reopened-with-v3.4), busy (win11refs2tmillionsofactionsv2, vclocks 293/292), and pre-salvage (vclocks 662/662) images. Only the virtual clock + per-page checksums differ. So checkpoint comparison yields NO prior whole-tree state on cleanly-unmounted-or-remountable volumes; a genuine mid-transaction crash capture (absent from corpus) is required. CAVEAT: comparing raw CHKP bytes is misleading (clock/checksum always differ) — must decode + compare root page-refs (parse_chkp). Recoverable prior states on a clean volume come from snapshots (#321) + superseded pages (deletion_recovery), not the alternate checkpoint. Confirms copy_on_write.md

description: Resident files have NO Object-Table OID. A resident file's dir-entry value+0x08 ("target_oid") is a VERSION-DEPENDENT sentinel (NOT in obj_map) whose high nibble tracks the value-format era: v3.4=0x18000000001, v3.7/3.9/3.10=0x1c000000001, v3.14=0x1e000000001 (disk-verified 2026-07-05: win10refsmini v3.4=0x18..., win11refs2g v3.14=0x1e...) — the file lives entirely inline in the parent directory's type-0x30 row, addressable only by (parent_oid, filename) = path. Non-resident DIRECTORIES get their own OID + B+-tree (value+0x08 = the directory's own OID); non-resident FILES do NOT — value+0x08 is the home-dir backref (the OID of the directory the file was first created in, a DIFFERENT object shared by all of a file's hard-link names; #340/FN_LINK_002/master 813/#335), and a file's content lives in type-0x40 backing/extent records reached via (home backref, child ordinal), not an Object-Table OID of its own. CORRECTED 2026-06-27: the prior 'Non-resident files/dirs DO get their own OID + B+-tree' over-generalised (conflated non-resident files with non-resident dirs) — this is the parser artifact behind the forefst/refsanalysis --oid file<->dir OID collision (a file reported with a directory's OID), fixed in the tools 2026-06-27 (analysis/reports/forefst_oid_bug_2026-06-27/). Confirmed across the corpus (every resident file = 0x1e000000001). Tool impact: added `details <path>` (forefst resolve_path + cmd_details) to give full details of resident files by path since `--oid` cannot reach them; offsets verified against the lister 15/15. Also added `snapshots --file/--extract` (export a file's snapshot versions), `deleted --extract` (export recovered deleted/old rows), and a summary upgrade flag (CHKP native-bit 0x080 + $VolInfo stamp ⇒ "APPEARS UPGRADED"). 234/234 matrix tests pass on 26 images

description: MLog redo-opcode table routing: v3.7–v3.13 must use the V314 table, not V34. On-disk evidence (win1121h2test v3.7, win1122h2test v3.9, win1123h2test v3.10) shows v3.7+ all emit the v3.14 opcode SUBSET — including the 0x1D–0x1F stream ops absent from v3.4 — and never use the version-ambiguous 0x16/0x17, so V314 names them correctly. The tool's `vmin >= 9` boundary mis-routed v3.7 to V34, leaving 78 of 3990 records (the 0x1F = RedoUpdateStreamSummary ones) unresolved. Fixed to `vmin >= 7` (helper `_redo_ops_for_version`); RD-validated to 0 unknown opcodes across all 83 corpus images with an MLog log. Cross-source check (item 2): USN file_oids overlap live $SI objects 75–100% (the remainder are deleted files preserved in the USN journal); MLog's `object_id` is a table/path namespace (not the file-OID), so the super-timeline correctly joins MLog by path, USN/$SI by OID. No inconsistencies found. (No v3.7–v3.10 driver exists to decompile; routing is RD-grounded.)

description: Primary and backup boot sectors can DIVERGE in the version field, and the driver cannot detect it. ReFS reads sector 0 (primary) then the last LBA (backup) at mount (RefsReadBootSector win11 @135981 / ReadBootSectorForMount insider @231636; backup LBA = totalSectors−1 = `*(VCB+0x1f0)−1`). Validation `RefsIsBootSectorOurs` (win11 @483985) is self-contained — signature + the ROR1+ADD u16 self-checksum at 0x16 (exactly `forefst._vbr_checksum`, now confirmed against the driver) + geometry sanity (0x18/0x20/0x24). There is NO cross-copy CRC64 and NO virtual clock, so the two copies' relative currency is undecidable; the backup is consulted only when the primary read physically FAILS (the fallback loop breaks on read success regardless of validity). Forensic consequences (RD): (a) on a genuine upgrade the backup retains the ORIGINAL pre-upgrade version — confirmed on TWO independent real upgrades: win10to11refs4g_afterwin11mount (primary v3.14 / backup v3.4) and win1122h2test_afteropenedwithinsider (v3.14 / v3.9); only bytes 0x29 (minor) + 0x17 (checksum) differ. This recovers the EXACT original version (vs $VolInfo's "pre-v3.10"). (b) The same divergence reveals VBR tampering: on hand-edited test images the primary carries the altered version (6.66, 3.15) while the backup holds the authentic 3.14. Static caveat (mechanism undetermined): the in-driver RW-mount upgrade dual-writes both copies (RefsWriteBootSector @135517, gated only by the SMR flag CmsVol+0xd34&0x800000), so both *should* be rewritten — yet the backup keeps the old version on every observed upgrade. The offline-tool hypothesis is ruled out: refsutil has no version-upgrade command (applets are dedup/snapshot/salvage/rollback/compression/triage/fixleaks/fixboot/iometrics), and its sole VBR writer `CFixBoot::Execute` (@140032770) also dual-writes primary + backup (decompiled). So the divergence seems to originate inside the driver's own upgrade path — the backup write apparently skipped on the upgrade transaction, or the version stamped via a sector-0-only path — but the exact reason is not pinned down. The empirical signal (backup retains the original version on 2 independent upgrades) is solid; the mechanism is not. Surfaced by `integrity` (Redundancy section reports the version mismatch + both versions, honestly "upgrade or tampering").

description: Deleted directory entries are recoverable in B+-tree NODE SLACK — and no current tool method gets them. Both tools' B+-tree iterators drive strictly off the live row-offset array `[thoff+astart, thoff+aend)`; the ~13 KB of slack in a 16 KB page is never read. Deletion (`CmsBPlusTable::DeleteFromIndex` @14001a610) removes only the offset-array slot and queues *delayed* compaction — row bytes are not scrubbed; they persist until a later CoW rewrite. RD (verified by hand): a slack scan of directory pages on win11refs2tmillionsofactions recovered 179 stale type-0x30 rows whose names are not in the live tree (real deleted names: xbpt_backup_report_125235.md, elvis_dir_zulu_272755, FVE2.{…}). `deleted --scan-pages` visits the right pages but still only follows their live offset array, so it misses these. BUILT as `deleted --slack` (v3.3.3, deletion-recovery "Method 5"): brute-walks free-region row headers, decodes name+MACB+attrs, confidence-graded (high/medium/partial by timestamp sanity), cross-flags deleted vs prior-version. Validated 0 false positives on the clean baseline (all 19 recovered names genuinely absent from the live tree); robust v3.4/64K/corrupt. (Q8)

description: The checkpoint Container-Table failover pair (root indices 7 & 8) is NOT bound to a fixed index→Table-ID order. Subtable A.4b lists root 7 = Container Table (ID 0x0B) / root 8 = duplicate (ID 0x0C); the claim-audit (probe `chkp_root_table`, owning-table id read at the table-root MSB+ page header +0x48) confirmed this on 106/110 images across many independent groups, but the 2 TB `win11refs2tmillionsofactions` volume carries the pair SWAPPED (root 7 → 0x0C, root 8 → 0x0B). The swap is present in the `_beforesalvage` image, so it is not a `refsutil salvage` artifact — the index→id binding of the failover pair is simply not guaranteed; the true invariant is the SET {0x0B,0x0C} at roots {7,8}. On the same volume root 12 (Small Allocator, expected ID 0x22) resolves to a Container-Table page (+0x48 = 0x0B) — a separate large-volume allocator-root anomaly, mechanism UNDETERMINED (open question). Same volume + 3 derivatives = 1 independent group; this refines A.4b (which shows only the common ordering) rather than overturning it. Surfaced by the P1 claim-audit (`forefstdev/docs/audit/`, FS_CHKP_016/017/021 scoped exceptions).

description: Non-resident file_attrs: the type-0x30 directory-entry POINTER (value+0x40) is an INCOMPLETE copy that OMITS the EA bit (0x40000); the authoritative attrs are in the type-0x40 backing record (value+0x48, the file's own $SI). Across all sampled images the ONLY attribute bits that ever differ between pointer(+0x40) and size-matched backing(+0x48) are 0x40000 (EA) and the transient 0x20 (Archive). Tools must read +0x48 (or OR-in its EA bit) to report HasEA/FileAttributes correctly for non-resident files. Resident files unaffected (+0x48 already authoritative). See E56.
static_analysis_notes: The type-0x40 backing IS the file's own $SI (#327/FN_LINK_002; same layout as the resident type-0x30 value, MD_SI_SA_001). EA-bit meaning per MD_ATTR_RA_003.
raw_analysis_notes: All-disk re-measure 2026-06-30 (broad 4k/64k v3.4/v3.14 + winsider/specials/insider): XOR(pointer+0x40, size-matched backing+0x48) over every non-resident file = exactly {0x40000, 0x20}; 0x40000 on 8140 files (winsider system files w/ $CI.CATALOGHINT/$KERNEL.PURGE EAs), 0x20 on 42 (sporadic, both directions; strict unique-size-match). forefst fixed 2026-06-29, refsanalysis 2026-06-30.

description: Type-0x40 backing record = the non-resident file's own $SI/stream-summary, keyed (owner-dir OID, file_id) and resolved by size-match (E51). Value field map: +0x48 file_attrs (AUTHORITATIVE), +0x4C internal flags, +0x50 SecurityId, +0x58 file size, +0x60 allocated size, +0x68 LastUsn ($SI+0x40), +0x70 UsnJournalId (volume epoch when set, else 0), +0x7C ReparseTag mirror (valid if bit31). Same layout as the resident type-0x30 value. structure_reference Subtable C.3a.
static_analysis_notes: Backing = file's own $SI (#327/FN_LINK_002; field semantics per the $SI map, MD_SI_SA_001).
raw_analysis_notes: RD-confirmed 2026-06-30 (winsider): SecurityId real (e.g. 8375163451), allocated cluster-aligned >= size, UsnJournalId constant 0x1dce1857f465fdd across files, +0x7C tag 0 on non-reparse. Drives forefst H3 columns (GroupSid/AllocatedSize/ReparseTag) + the non-resident EA/USN/SecurityId enrichment.

description: Non-resident reparse storage: the REPARSE_DATA_BUFFER is embedded in the file's type-0x40 backing record (the (owner-dir, file_id) record), and the tag is mirrored at backing +0x7C. v3.14: the target buffer is recovered from the file's own (home) backing. v3.7-v3.10: the backing carries ONLY the tag mirror; the target buffer lives in CoW-old pages, not the live tree (OPEN). (Resident reparse files embed the buffer in the type-0xC0 sub-record at value+0x0C; see C.8 Reparse Storage.)
static_analysis_notes: RefsGetReparsePointValue -> attribute 0xC0 -> FsRtlValidateReparsePointBuffer. Tag mirror @ $SI+0x54 / backing+0x7C (E21).
raw_analysis_notes: v3.14 target recovered from the home backing (forefst H3 non-res ReparseTarget; collision-free oracle 375/0). v3.7-v3.10 mirror-only confirmed (target absent from the live tree). Resident buffer @ type-0xC0 v+0x0C disk-confirmed on 5 reparse files (win11refs2gtargeted, 2026-06-30).

description: Per-name MACB divergence across hard-link names — a ReFS-only, journal-independent timestomp cross-check. $SI MACB is stored PER NAME (per hard link), not per inode as on NTFS: each name is an independent type-0x30 non-resident row with its own Created(value+0x10)/Modified(+0x18)/Changed(+0x20)/Accessed(+0x28). A name-scoped SetFileTime rewrites only the opened name AND the shared type-0x40 backing; sibling names keep the true birth, so the LATEST Created among siblings is authentic and a backdated name is the stomp. Stronger than NTFS $SI-vs-$FN (NTFS hard links share one $SI and cannot diverge). See E59; supersedes the '#335 one-timestamp-set / no cross-check' framing for hard-linked files.
static_analysis_notes: Per-name type-0x30 rows + shared type-0x40 backing per FN_LINK_002/MD_DATA_RA_012 (E2 layout); the SetFileTime propagation-to-opened-name-only path was not decompiled here (RD-established).
raw_analysis_notes: win11refs8g.raw (step6), File ID 0x1e (home 0x746): after Get-Item R:\blabla\f6.bin; $i.CreationTimeUtc='2011-01-01Z', the two hard-link names read f6.bin Created 0x01cba946d534c000=2011-01-01 vs f6_link.bin Created 0x01dd0b1d102d451c=2026-07-03 18:52:14 (true birth); shared type-0x40 backing value+0x28=2011-01-01 (matches the stomped name, not the clean sibling). USN journal corroborates (FILE_CREATE 2026-07-03) but is NOT required. forefst timestomp emits HARDLINK_MACB_MISMATCH, flagging only the backdated name (f6_link.bin correctly NOT flagged).
